title: Angular Advanced — Paper 1
description: Advanced Angular concepts: reactive forms, custom validators, routing, guards, HTTP client, RxJS, change detection, custom directives/pipes, state management, and testing.

questions.text: Which method on FormGroup returns the form value INCLUDING controls that are disabled?
An async validator function must return which of the following?

options.text: An Observable or Promise that resolves to ValidationErrors | null
A Subject that emits validation state

questions.text: In Angular routing, what is the difference between RouterModule.forRoot() and RouterModule.forChild()?

options.text: forRoot creates the router outlet; forChild does not
forRoot registers the router service and directives app-wide; forChild registers only routes without re-providing the router service
forRoot is used in lazy modules; forChild is used in the root module
They are identical; the names are just conventions

questions.text: Given route config: { path: 'product/:id', component: ProductComponent }, how do you read the 'id' param in the component using ActivatedRoute?
Which guard interface would you implement to prevent a user from navigating AWAY from a form with unsaved changes?
What is the purpose of ResolveFn in Angular routing?

options.text: Pre-fetches data before a route is activated so the component receives it immediately
Redirects the user if they are not authenticated
Matches routes based on custom logic

questions.text: Which HttpClient method should you use to send data and receive a response body typed as User?
What does switchMap do differently from mergeMap when handling inner Observables?

options.text: switchMap queues inner observables; mergeMap cancels them
switchMap cancels the previous inner observable when a new outer value arrives; mergeMap lets all inner observables run concurrently
switchMap runs inner observables sequentially; mergeMap cancels all but the last

questions.text: Which RxJS operator would you use to combine the latest values from two observables and emit whenever EITHER emits?
What is the main benefit of using ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush on a component?

options.text: The component is checked on every browser event
Change detection only runs when @Input references change, an event fires inside the component, or an async pipe emits
The component is excluded from the change detection tree entirely
It enables two-way data binding automatically

questions.text: What is the purpose of NgZone.runOutsideAngular()?

options.text: To execute code that should trigger Angular change detection
To execute code without triggering Angular change detection, improving performance for non-UI tasks
To destroy the Angular zone entirely
Changes the host element's background color to orange on mouseenter and back to yellow on mouseleave
Adds a CSS class on hover

questions.text: What is the difference between a pure and an impure pipe in Angular?

options.text: A pure pipe runs on every change detection cycle; an impure pipe runs only when the input reference changes
A pure pipe runs only when the input reference or primitive value changes; an impure pipe runs on every change detection cycle
Pure pipes cannot be used with async data
Impure pipes are deprecated in Angular 17+

questions.text: In NgRx, what does createSelector() return?

options.text: A memoized selector function that derives state slices from the store

questions.text: What is the role of createEffect() in NgRx?

options.text: Registers a side-effect listener that reacts to dispatched actions and optionally dispatches new actions
Defines a selector for component-level state

questions.text: In Angular Signals, what does computed() do?

options.text: Creates a read-only signal that automatically recomputes when its dependent signals change
Subscribes to an observable and returns a signal
Schedules a side effect when signals change

questions.text: Which Angular testing utility is used to simulate the passage of time in fakeAsync tests?
What does TestBed.configureTestingModule() do in an Angular unit test?

options.text: Compiles all application components for E2E tests
Sets up an Angular testing module with declarations, imports, and providers needed for the test
Creates a Jasmine spy on every service method
Bootstraps the real application in a headless browser
Eager loading of the admin module
Lazy loading — the admin module is only loaded when the 'admin' route is accessed
Pre-loading the admin module at startup

questions.text: In an HttpInterceptorFn, which argument provides access to the original HTTP request?
What is a BehaviorSubject different from a regular Subject in RxJS?

options.text: BehaviorSubject holds and replays the current value to new subscribers; a Subject does not
BehaviorSubject completes automatically; Subject never completes
Subject is synchronous; BehaviorSubject is always asynchronous

questions.text: What does the trackBy function do in an *ngFor directive?

options.text: Provides a unique identifier so Angular can reuse existing DOM elements instead of recreating all of them when the list changes
Enables virtual scrolling for large lists

questions.text: In Angular cross-field validation, where should you attach the validator to compare two form controls (e.g., password vs confirmPassword)?

options.text: On the parent FormGroup so the validator has access to both controls
As a template validator using ngModel

questions.text: Which DomSanitizer method should you use to safely bind a trusted URL to an iframe src?
What is the purpose of toSignal() in Angular?

options.text: Converts a signal into an observable
Converts an observable into a signal so it can be used in signal-based templates
Creates a computed signal from multiple inputs
Schedules a signal update outside the Angular zone

questions.text: In NgRx, what is the correct way to define a reducer using createReducer?

options.text: const reducer = createReducer(initialState, on(myAction, (state) => ({ ...state, loaded: true })));
const reducer = new Reducer(initialState, myAction, (state) => state);
const reducer = (state = initialState, action) => { switch(action.type) { default: return state; } };
